steps.title: Follow the time shift
steps.content: The text begins with Dolly Parton's recordings in the late 1970s and first half of the 1980s, especially the country and pop influences in Here You Come Again. It then shifts with the phrase A decade earlier to discuss an earlier part of her career.

steps.title: Name the career structure
steps.content: The earlier period is described as rooted in traditional folk music of the Blue Ridge Mountains, with Jolene used as an example of that influence. Choice A invents a debate about pop influence. Choice C adds praise of early recordings and disappointment with later ones. Choice D claims the two songs are remarkably similar, which the passage does not say. The answer is B.
